subject: Just received my order — absolutely delighted!
body:
Hi,

I just received my parcel and I honestly couldn't be happier. The product quality is exceptional — better than the photos suggested — and it arrived a full day ahead of schedule. I've already shared the campaign link with my sister and two colleagues. Will definitely be ordering again.

Warm regards,
Alice
notes: Unambiguous enthusiastic positive — baseline sanity check

subject: Third order this year and still impressed
body:
Hello,

I've now ordered three times through your campaigns and the quality has been consistently excellent. This latest purchase is no exception. Packaging was great, delivery was on time, and the product is exactly what was described. Keep it up!

Raj
notes: Repeat customer satisfaction — should score high positive

subject: Campaign discount — great deal, great product
body:
Hi there,

I was initially sceptical about the 35% discount but I took the plunge and I'm really glad I did. Product arrived in 3 days, quality is solid, and it does exactly what it says on the tin. Genuinely good value for money.

Emma
notes: Satisfied but measured — good value, not ecstatic

subject: Re: Summer Sale — bought as a gift, huge success
body:
Hi,

I bought this as a birthday gift during the campaign and my partner absolutely loved it. Great quality, beautiful packaging, and delivered on exactly the right day. You've made both of us very happy customers.

Liam
notes: Gift purchase — happiness expressed on behalf of two people

subject: Ordered for the whole team — everyone's thrilled
body:
Hello,

I placed a bulk order for our team of eight during the campaign and everyone has been delighted. The consistency of quality across all items was impressive. Several colleagues have already asked me for the link to order for themselves.

Priya
notes: Team/bulk order — strong advocacy signal

subject: Already reordered — best campaign you've done
body:
Hi,

I placed my second order before I'd even finished the first one. The product is that good. Delivery was fast, the quality rivals things I've paid twice as much for elsewhere. This is the best campaign you've run. Great job.

Ben
notes: Immediate reorder — strongest positive buying behaviour signal

subject: Order status enquiry
body:
Hi,

I placed order #847291 six days ago and haven't received a shipping confirmation yet. I'm not worried — I just want to make sure everything is on track. Could you let me know the current status?

Thanks,
Tom
notes: Polite status check — explicitly says 'not worried', no frustration

subject: Return policy for campaign purchases?
body:
Hello,

I bought two items during the campaign and I'd like to return one as it doesn't quite fit my needs — nothing wrong with the product itself. Could you clarify the return window for campaign orders and the process?

Thanks,
Sofia
notes: Return request — product fine, just wrong fit, no negative emotion

subject: Delivery estimate for my order?
body:
Hi,

I placed my order five days ago and was given an estimated delivery of 4–6 days. Could you give me an updated delivery estimate? I need to make sure someone is home to receive it.

Victor
notes: Practical delivery question — no frustration, just logistics

subject: Order arrived — will review once I've tried it
body:
Hi,

My order arrived today. Packaging looked fine and everything was in the box. I haven't had a chance to try it properly yet so I'll reserve judgement, but first impressions are okay. I'll send a proper review once I've used it.

Chloe
notes: Deliberately non-committal — 'okay', no strong signal either way

subject: Question about product specs before I order
body:
Hello,

I'm interested in ordering through the campaign but I have a quick question about the product dimensions — the listing doesn't specify. Could you confirm the exact measurements? I want to make sure it fits my space before I commit.

Kevin
notes: Pre-purchase information request — interested but uncommitted

subject: Shipping address correction
body:
Hi,

I just placed order #913044 and realised I entered the wrong postcode. Could you update the delivery address to: 14 Maple Close, Bristol, BS8 2QW? Apologies for the trouble.

Anna
notes: Administrative request — apologetic tone, no emotional content

subject: Product not as described — very disappointed
body:
Hello,

I received my order and the product looks nothing like the campaign photos. The colour is completely different and the finish feels cheap. I specifically chose this based on the images and description, so I feel misled. I'd like to understand my options.

David
notes: Disappointed but measured — misled by photos, no escalation yet

subject: Delivery now 10 days overdue
body:
Hi,

My estimated delivery was 5 days. It has now been 15 days and I still have no update. I've checked the tracking page and it hasn't moved in a week. This is causing me real inconvenience and I need this resolved urgently.

Grace
notes: Overdue delivery — frustrated but not yet threatening action

subject: Item arrived damaged
body:
Hello,

My order arrived this morning and one of the items is damaged — there's a crack along the base that wasn't caused by transit, the packaging was intact. This is clearly a quality control issue. I'd like a replacement sent as soon as possible.

James
notes: Damaged product — clear complaint, expects resolution, medium urgency

subject: Wrong item received
body:
Hi,

I ordered the blue version of the product and received the green one instead. Order #762109. I understand mistakes happen but I need the correct item. Could you arrange a return and send the right one? This is frustrating.

Mia
notes: Fulfillment error — frustrated but still cooperative

subject: Three support tickets, zero responses
body:
Hello,

I have sent three separate support requests over the past two weeks regarding a defective product and I have not received a single response. Not even an acknowledgement. This level of service is completely unacceptable. Please contact me today.

Ethan
notes: Ignored by support — strong frustration, repeated attempts

subject: Missing item from my order
body:
Hi,

I ordered three items and only two arrived. The third — the medium size — wasn't in the box. The packing slip shows all three were supposedly included. Please send the missing item or issue a partial refund for it.

Natalie
notes: Missing item — clear problem, requesting resolution, not angry yet

subject: Quality below expectations — regret this purchase
body:
Hi,

I'm genuinely disappointed. The campaign made the product look premium but what arrived feels flimsy and cheaply made. For this price — even with the discount — I expect much better. I wouldn't recommend it to anyone based on what I've received.

Linda
notes: Quality disappointment — negative but no escalation, just regret

subject: Defective product — want a replacement
body:
Hello,

The product stopped working after two uses. The mechanism jams and won't release. This is clearly a manufacturing defect. I'd like a replacement unit sent out. If that's not possible, I'll need a full refund. Please respond within 48 hours.

Chris
notes: Defective product with deadline — medium urgency (48h ask, not legal threat)

subject: Re: Campaign order — a small concern
body:
Hi,

I don't like to complain and I'm sure the campaign was well-intentioned, but the product quality really isn't what I was hoping for. It looks fine but feels very lightweight and I'm not sure it will last. I was expecting something more substantial based on the listing.

Olivia
notes: Polite framing ('I don't like to complain') but clearly disappointed — must not be read as neutral

subject: Possibly my mistake but product doesn't match listing
body:
Hi there,

I'm sure it's probably my own error but the product I received doesn't seem to match what was shown in the campaign. The dimensions are noticeably smaller than the listing suggests. I'd appreciate some clarification — if it's a genuine discrepancy I'd like to discuss options.

Sarah
notes: Self-deprecating framing ('probably my mistake') masking a legitimate complaint

subject: Thank you for the campaign — though I must say
body:
Dear team,

Thank you for running this campaign — I appreciate the effort. That said, I must be honest: the product didn't quite live up to my expectations. The build quality feels a step below what the price point implies, even with the discount applied. A little disappointed overall.

Mike
notes: Opens with gratitude then reveals disappointment — the positive opener must not dominate

subject: Re: Support ticket #4412
body:
Oh wonderful. Another automated acknowledgement. How incredibly helpful. I raised this issue nine days ago and I've received three auto-replies and zero actual assistance. I'm sure a human will get to it eventually — presumably sometime next quarter.

Dan
notes: Heavy sarcasm — 'Oh wonderful', 'incredibly helpful' are all negative. Must not be read as positive.

subject: Express delivery, certainly
body:
Hi,

I ordered eleven days ago. Your site promised 3–5 day express delivery. Eleven days. I'm sure 'express' means something different in your world. Still no update on tracking. Remarkable service.

Jess
notes: Sarcastic framing — 'certainly', 'remarkable service' are ironic. Sentiment is strongly negative.

subject: Great product, terrible experience
body:
Hi,

Honestly the product itself is lovely — really happy with the quality. But the delivery took three weeks when five days was promised, and getting any update was impossible. The product earns 5 stars; the logistics and communication earn 1. Overall I'm left frustrated.

Ethan
notes: Genuine mixed sentiment — the explicit 'left frustrated' and 3-week delay should tip it negative

subject: Good product, poor support
body:
Hi,

The product quality is fine and the price with the discount is fair. However I emailed support twice about a small query and was ignored both times. It's a minor thing but being ignored doesn't leave a good impression. Mixed feelings overall.

Priya
notes: Product ok but ignored by support — 'mixed feelings' but ignored support tips it negative

subject: Love the product, hate the packaging
body:
Hello,

The product itself is fantastic and I'm really pleased with it. But the packaging was terrible — everything was loose in a box that was too big, and two items arrived scratched. They still work fine but it felt careless. Please improve your packaging.

Anna
notes: Genuinely balanced — strong positive on product, clear negative on packaging, roughly even

subject: Re: Order #990211
body: Disgusted.
notes: One-word reply — extremely negative tone even without context. Urgency medium due to intensity.

subject: Re: Your summer campaign
body: Perfect, thank you! Arrived quickly and exactly as described. Very happy.
notes: Three-sentence positive — short but clearly positive, no ambiguity

subject: Still waiting
body: Two weeks in. Still waiting. Not great.
notes: Short terse complaint — negative but no explicit escalation threat

subject: Re: Support ticket #7801 (still open)
body:
Hi,

I'm sure your support team is very busy and I completely understand that. I will simply wait for my fourth response. No rush at all. It's only my money and my time.

James
notes: Passive-aggressive — 'I'm sure', 'no rush', 'only my money' are all loaded with frustration

subject: No worries, take your time
body:
No worries, honestly. Take all the time you need. It's only been three weeks since I placed the order. I'm sure it will arrive eventually. No rush whatsoever.

Mia
notes: All-positive words, completely sarcastic meaning — must not be read as positive or neutral

subject: FULL REFUND REQUIRED — final request before bank dispute
body:
This is my final request before I contact my bank to dispute this charge. The product is defective, you have ignored four support tickets, and it has been over three weeks. Issue a full refund to my card within 24 hours or I will initiate a chargeback.

Kevin Osei
notes: Explicit chargeback threat with deadline — unmistakably high urgency

subject: Small claims court — last chance
body:
I have documented every interaction: five unanswered emails, a defective product, and a charge that has not been refunded. If this is not resolved by end of business tomorrow I will file a claim in small claims court. I have already consulted the process.

Grace Kim
notes: Explicit legal (small claims court) threat — clearly high urgency

subject: Complaint filed with consumer authority
body:
I have today filed a formal complaint with the consumer protection authority regarding this purchase. The product was falsely advertised and your customer service has been non-existent. I am also leaving detailed reviews on every platform I can access.

David Okafor
notes: Regulatory complaint already filed + review threats — high urgency

subject: Sharing this with my 14,000 followers unless resolved today
body:
I have 14,000 followers on Instagram and a further 8,000 on TikTok. I have already drafted a detailed post about my experience with this campaign. I will publish it tonight unless I receive a resolution — full refund + apology — by 5pm today.

Natalie Chen
notes: Large-following social media threat with explicit deadline — highest urgency reputational risk

subject: FINAL NOTICE
body:
This is your final notice. I have been patient for four weeks. The product is broken, my refund has not arrived, and your team has ignored every message. If I do not receive a full refund confirmation by tomorrow morning I will pursue every legal avenue available to me.

Ben Walker
notes: FINAL NOTICE + legal threat — very high urgency

subject: Chargeback initiated
body:
I have just called American Express and initiated a chargeback for this transaction. I gave you four weeks and multiple opportunities to resolve this. The case is now with my bank. I will also be reporting this to Trading Standards.

Olivia Brooks
notes: Chargeback already initiated — high urgency, past the warning stage

subject: Legal advice obtained regarding misleading campaign
body:
I have spoken with a consumer rights solicitor regarding the misleading claims in this campaign. I have been advised that I have grounds to pursue compensation. I am giving you 48 hours to make this right before I instruct my solicitor to proceed.

Sarah Klein
notes: Lawyer already consulted, explicit 48h ultimatum — high urgency
